title: AgentRuntime Docs
excerpt: AgentRuntime Docs Stop experimenting. Put your agents to work. Learn how to design workflows, connect tools via MCP, run agents reliably, and govern access across your workspace. What is AgentRuntime?

title: Quickstart
excerpt: This guide walks you through the essential setup in the [AgentRuntime Console](https://console.agentruntime.io): create a workspace, invite a teammate, connect an integration, and run your first workf

title: Key concepts
excerpt: Understanding the core model makes every other guide easier to follow. This page explains the foundational concepts you will encounter throughout AgentRuntime. ## Workspaces (tenants) A **workspace**

title: Workspace settings
excerpt: Workspace settings control how your organization is identified, how members authenticate, and how billing is managed. Navigate to **Settings** in the [Console](https://console.agentruntime.io) to acce

title: User management
excerpt: Workspace admins manage the member lifecycle from **Settings → Members** in the [Console](https://console.agentruntime.io): invite new users, adjust roles, and deactivate accounts. ## Invite by email

title: Roles and permissions
excerpt: AgentRuntime uses role-based access control (RBAC). Roles are assigned at the **tenant** (workspace) level and at the **project** level. Project roles govern most day-to-day Console and API access. ##

title: Billing and credits
excerpt: AgentRuntime meters usage in **credits**. Workflows, memory indexing, and other billable actions debit your workspace balance. Manage billing from **Settings** (`/settings`) in the [Console](https://c

title: Multi-product billing
excerpt: AgentRuntime's billing kernel (**Wheelhouse**) can host **multiple commercial products** on one database. Each product has its own plan catalog, trial policies, and workspace lineage — keyed by **`pro

title: Analytics
excerpt: **Analytics** (`/analytics`) summarizes how your workflows perform and what they cost. Open it from the Console sidebar when **Analytics** is enabled for your deployment. Analytics nav is feature-flag

title: Security and compliance
excerpt: AgentRuntime is a multi-tenant platform for running AI agent workflows. This page summarizes security practices for builders and enterprise evaluators. It is an overview — not a contractual commitment

title: Referrals and affiliates
excerpt: AgentRuntime runs **two related programs**: 1. **Referrals** (`/referrals`) — Self-service for promoters who share codes with friends 2. **Friend referral credits** — Bonus workspace credits when a ne

title: Feature availability
excerpt: Some capabilities are fully supported in the Console; others are API-only, feature-flagged, or catalog placeholders. This page is the single reference — individual guides link here instead of repeatin

title: Troubleshooting
excerpt: This guide covers the most common operator issues in AgentRuntime. Start from [Command Center](/workflows/command-center) for run-level triage, then use the sections below by symptom. ## Failed runs #

title: Workflow Studio
excerpt: **Workflow Studio** is the visual editor for designing agent workflows. Open it from the Console sidebar at **Workflow Studio** (`/workflow`). ## The authoring loop Click **New workflow** and give it

title: Template intellisense
excerpt: Workflow Studio helps you connect steps with **template variables** — placeholders like `{{input.topic}}` or `{{steps. .result.message}}` that fill in at run time from start input or upstream step out

title: Run setup and start input
excerpt: **Run setup** is how you choose **what input a workflow run starts with** — before any step executes. In Workflow Studio, open **Workflow configuration → Trigger**. Run setup is separate from the canv

title: External triggers and Run setup
excerpt: External systems start workflows **without** Workflow Studio. Saved **Run setup** defaults apply only when explicitly enabled — the default is **off** so callers are not surprised by Studio-saved valu

title: Step types
excerpt: Workflow graphs are made of typed **steps**. Each step has an `id`, `type`, `name`, optional `depends_on`, and type-specific fields. The JSON schema is published at [agentruntime.io/schemas/workflow-g

title: Command Center
excerpt: **Command Center** is your workspace-wide run inbox. Open it from the Console sidebar at **Command Center** (`/command-center`). Use it when you need to approve human tasks, pause or stop runs, or tri

title: Runs and Command Center
excerpt: A **run** is one execution of a published workflow version. This guide covers run lifecycle, controls, observability, and the Command Center inbox. ## Starting a run Start runs from: - **Workflow Stud

title: Run recovery (roadmap)
excerpt: **Status:** Failed runs are **terminal** today. This page describes current operator workflow and **planned** recovery features. When a step fails, see [Runs and Command Center — Failure handling](/wo

title: Human tasks
excerpt: **Human tasks** let you pause a workflow until a person reviews, approves, rejects, or edits data. They are essential for production agents that take real-world actions — sending emails, updating CRM

title: Workflow patterns
excerpt: These patterns combine step types, Command Center, and integrations into workflows you can adapt for production. See the [JSON cookbook](/workflows/json-schema-cookbook) for full graph examples. ## Ap

title: Workflow JSON cookbook
excerpt: Workflow graphs are JSON documents describing a directed acyclic graph of steps. Author them in **Workflow Studio** or submit them via `POST /v1/workflows`. **JSON Schema:** [agentruntime.io/schemas/w

title: End-to-end guides
excerpt: These guides walk through **complete workflows** you can build in the Console. Each one connects multiple MCP adapters, shows per-step JSON, includes a **full workflow graph** for copy-paste, and link

title: Form webhook → Postgres → Resend digest

title: Connections

title: MCP instances

title: Custom MCP servers

title: Google Workspace

title: Integration authentication
excerpt: Connectors use different authentication methods. AgentRuntime stores credentials in **connections** and injects them at runtime through **MCP instance bindings** — never embed secrets in workflow grap

title: Inbound webhooks

title: API reference
excerpt: OpenAPI reference overview

title: AgentRuntime API - overview
excerpt:
## Overview

The AgentRuntime API lets you **register, execute, monitor, and manage AI agent workflows**
programmatically. Workflows are defined as directed acyclic graphs (DAGs) whose steps can call
MCP tool servers, invoke LLMs, pause for human review, or run sandboxed Lua transforms.


## Authentication

All endpoints require a Bearer token passed in the `Authorization` header:

```
Authorization: Bearer <your-api-key>
```

Obtain your API key from the AgentRuntime dashboard or waitlist onboarding email.


## Base URL

```
https://api.agentruntime.io
```


## Request / Response format

All request and response bodies are `application/json`. Dates are ISO 8601 strings.


## Async execution model

Workflow execution is **asynchronous**. `POST /workflows/{id}/command` with `"start"` returns
the initial run state immediately — the workflow continues running in the background. Use the
WebSocket stream (`GET /workflows/{id}/stream`) or poll `GET /workflows/{id}/runs` to track
progress.


## Step types

| Type | What it does |
|------|-------------|
| `mcp_call` | Calls a tool on a remote MCP server |
| `llm_call` | Sends a prompt to an LLM and captures the response |
| `human_task` | Pauses the workflow until a human submits a decision |
| `lua_script` | Runs a sandboxed Lua script inline — no external round-trip |


## Template variables

Step arguments can reference upstream results and the workflow's input payload:

```
{{steps.<step-id>.result.<field>}}   — result of a completed upstream step
{{input.<field>}}                    — value from the workflow start payload
```

Parallel execution emerges automatically: steps whose `depends_on` are all satisfied run
concurrently without any extra configuration.


## Error handling

All errors return a JSON body with `error` (human-readable) and optional `code` (machine-readable).
HTTP status codes follow REST conventions: 400 Bad Request, 401 Unauthorized, 404 Not Found,
409 Conflict, 422 Unprocessable Entity, 500 Internal Server Error.


## Rate limits

Rate limits are applied per API key. The `X-RateLimit-Remaining` and `X-RateLimit-Reset`
headers are included in every response.

title: AgentRuntime API - Workflows
excerpt:
Create, update, and delete workflow definitions. A workflow is a named, versioned JSON graph
of steps. Definitions are immutable once a run is active — update the definition to create a
new version.

title: GET /api/v1/workflows
excerpt: List workflows

title: POST /api/v1/workflows
excerpt: Create a workflow

title: GET /api/v1/workflows/{id}
excerpt: Get a workflow

title: PUT /api/v1/workflows/{id}
excerpt: Replace a workflow definition

title: PATCH /api/v1/workflows/{id}
excerpt: Partially update a workflow

title: DELETE /api/v1/workflows/{id}
excerpt: Delete a workflow

title: AgentRuntime API - Execution
excerpt:
Trigger and control workflow runs. Use `POST /command` to start, pause, resume, or cancel.
Use `GET /stream` for a WebSocket connection that pushes real-time events as the workflow executes.

title: POST /api/v1/workflows/{id}/command
excerpt: Execute a workflow command

title: GET /api/v1/workflows/{id}/stream
excerpt: Real-time event stream (WebSocket)

title: AgentRuntime API - Runs
excerpt:
Query run history, event logs, and context snapshots. Every action in a run produces an
immutable event — use the event log for debugging and audit.

title: GET /api/v1/workflows/{id}/runs
excerpt: List runs for a workflow

title: GET /api/v1/workflows/{id}/runs/{run_id}/events
excerpt: List events for a run

title: GET /api/v1/workflows/{id}/runs/{run_id}/context
excerpt: Get run context snapshot

title: AgentRuntime API - Tasks
excerpt:
Human-in-the-loop (HITL) task management. When a workflow reaches a `human_task` step it
pauses and creates a pending task. Submit a decision to resume execution.

title: GET /api/v1/workflows/{id}/runs/{run_id}/tasks/pending
excerpt: List pending human tasks

title: POST /api/v1/workflows/{id}/runs/{run_id}/tasks/{task_id}/complete
excerpt: Complete a human task

title: AgentRuntime API - MCP Tools
excerpt: List tool definitions discovered from registered MCP servers.

title: GET /api/v1/mcp/tools
excerpt: List available MCP tools

title: AgentRuntime API - Credentials
excerpt:
Manage per-MCP OAuth tokens and API keys. Credentials are stored in HashiCorp Vault and
cached in Redis. Updating a credential invalidates the cache automatically.

title: GET /api/v1/credentials/mcp
excerpt: List MCP credentials

title: POST /api/v1/credentials/mcp
excerpt: Create an MCP credential

title: PUT /api/v1/credentials/mcp/{secret_id}
excerpt: Rotate a credential

title: DELETE /api/v1/credentials/mcp/{secret_id}
excerpt: Delete a credential

title: AgentRuntime API - Builtin Tools
excerpt:
Query metadata about built-in tool capabilities provided by the runtime itself
(LLM invocation, human_task, etc.).

title: GET /api/v1/builtin-tools
excerpt: List built-in tool capabilities
